composer: Hidenori Maezawa, Jun Funahashi, Yukie Morimoto, Yoshinori Sasaki

attack.character: Grant throws daggers (Japanese version differs from Western stabbing dagger)

audio_identity.overall_style: Synthesized string section imitation using combined channels; gothic horror platformer aesthetic
distinctive_elements: VRC6 coprocessor with two extra pulse wave channels and saw wave channel (Japanese version)
Majority of music combines channels to imitate synthesized strings
Western versions remixed for MMC5 standard five channels by Yoshinori Sasaki
Multiple playable character themes corresponding to Trevor, Sypha, Grant, and Alucard

regional_differences.japanese_version: VRC6 coprocessor with 8 total channels, synthesized string effects, special effects not in Western releases, different backgrounds in many stages
regional_differences.western_versions: MMC5 chip with 5 standard channels, music remixed by Yoshinori Sasaki, censored enemy sprites and religious iconography
regional_differences.audio_impact: Japanese version features richer synthesized strings and additional channel capabilities; Western versions simplified for hardware compatibility

soundtrack_notes: Akumajō Dracula Famicom Best soundtrack album released featuring original Famicom version music
